basics.description:
## 🛡️ SpyCloud Sentinel — Unified Threat Intelligence

**Transform recaptured darknet data into automated identity threat protection.**

Deploy SpyCloud's breach, malware, and phishing intelligence into Microsoft Sentinel with:

- **CCF Data Connector** — 3 REST API pollers (Watchlist New + Modified + Catalog)
- **4 Custom Tables** — 73+ columns covering credentials, PII, device forensics, account metadata
- **2 Logic App Playbooks** — MDE device isolation + Conditional Access identity protection
- **17 Analytics Rules** — Infostealer, plaintext passwords, PII, session cookies, device re-infection, IdP correlation (all deploy DISABLED for review)
- **Azure Key Vault** — Secure API key storage
- **Automated Post-Deploy** — DCE/DCR resolution and RBAC assignment runs inside the deployment

**Prerequisites:** SpyCloud Enterprise API key from [portal.spycloud.com](https://portal.spycloud.com) → Settings → API Keys

[Documentation](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL) · [Architecture Guide](https://github.com/iammrherb/SPYCLOUD-SENTINEL/blob/main/docs/architecture.md)

options.text: You need a SpyCloud Enterprise API key. Get yours at portal.spycloud.com → Settings → API Keys.

elements.name: createNewWorkspace
elements.label: Workspace
elements.toolTip: Create a new workspace or use an existing one with Sentinel enabled.

elements.name: workspace
elements.label: Workspace Name
elements.toolTip: Name for new workspace, or exact name of existing workspace.

constraints.validationMessage: Must be 3-63 characters: letters, numbers, hyphens. Must start with a letter.

elements.name: retentionInDays
elements.label: Data Retention (days)
elements.toolTip: How long to retain SpyCloud data in Log Analytics. 90 days recommended.

elements.name: spycloudApiKey
elements.toolTip: Your SpyCloud Enterprise API key from portal.spycloud.com

elements.name: spycloudApiRegion
elements.label: SpyCloud API Region
elements.toolTip: Select the SpyCloud API endpoint region.

options.text: Configure which SpyCloud exposure data to ingest. Severity levels determine the type and urgency of exposed data. Higher severity = more critical exposure.

elements.name: severityFilter
elements.label: Severity Levels to Ingest
elements.toolTip:
Select which SpyCloud severity levels to ingest.

• Severity 2: Email + password from third-party breach (lowest risk)
• Severity 5: Breach credential + PII (name, phone, DOB, address)
• Severity 20: Infostealer credential — malware stole this from an infected device (HIGH risk)
• Severity 25: Infostealer + application data — stolen cookies, sessions, autofill enabling MFA bypass (CRITICAL)

elements.name: showPlainPassword
elements.label: Include Plaintext Passwords in Data
elements.toolTip:
Controls whether plaintext passwords are included in SpyCloud data ingested into Sentinel.

Set to 'No' (recommended) to redact all plaintext passwords before they reach your SIEM. This is required by many compliance frameworks (PCI DSS, SOC 2, HIPAA).

Set to 'Yes' only if your SOC team needs to see exposed passwords for investigation purposes and your organization's data handling policies permit it.

elements.name: queryWindowInMin
elements.label: Polling Interval (minutes)
elements.toolTip: How often the CCF connector polls the SpyCloud API for new data. Default: 40 minutes. Lower values increase freshness but may hit API rate limits.

elements.name: initialLookbackDays
elements.label: Initial Data Lookback (days)
elements.toolTip: How many days of historical SpyCloud data to pull on first connection. Default: 30 days. Higher values pull more historical data but take longer on initial sync.

options.text: Playbooks are Logic Apps that automatically respond to SpyCloud-detected threats. They use Sentinel automation rules with the 'Microsoft Sentinel Incident' trigger to fire when incidents are created. Each playbook uses a system-assigned managed identity — no credentials are stored in the workflow. API permissions (MDE Machine.Isolate, Graph User.ReadWrite.All) are assigned by the automated post-deploy script or can be granted manually in Azure Portal → Enterprise Applications.

elements.name: enableMdePlaybook
elements.label: Enable MDE Device Isolation Playbook
elements.toolTip:
Deploys a Logic App that automatically responds to infostealer infections detected by SpyCloud:

1. Triggered by Sentinel incident (severity 20+)
2. Extracts infected_machine_id and user_hostname from SpyCloud data
3. Searches Microsoft Defender for Endpoint for matching devices
4. If match found: isolates the device to prevent lateral movement
5. Tags the device with 'SpyCloud-Infostealer' for SOC tracking
6. Adds investigation comments to the Sentinel incident
7. Logs all actions to Spycloud_MDE_Logs_CL audit table

Requires: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint license, Machine.Isolate + Machine.ReadWrite.All API permissions.

elements.name: enableCaPlaybook
elements.label: Enable Conditional Access Remediation Playbook
elements.toolTip:
Deploys a Logic App that protects compromised identities when SpyCloud detects credential exposure:

1. Triggered by Sentinel incident (any severity with email field)
2. Extracts compromised email addresses from SpyCloud data
3. Looks up user in Entra ID (Azure AD) via Microsoft Graph
4. Forces password reset on next sign-in
5. Revokes all active sessions and refresh tokens
6. Optionally adds user to a Conditional Access exclusion group
7. Adds remediation comments to the Sentinel incident
8. Logs all actions to SpyCloud_ConditionalAccessLogs_CL audit table

Requires: Entra ID P1+, User.ReadWrite.All + Directory.ReadWrite.All Graph API permissions.

elements.name: enableKeyVault
elements.label: Store API key in Azure Key Vault
elements.toolTip: Creates an Azure Key Vault and stores the SpyCloud API key as a secret. Recommended for production deployments.

elements.name: enableAnalyticsRule
elements.label: Enable primary infostealer detection rule
elements.toolTip: Creates the primary analytics rule that detects severity 20+ infostealer exposures and creates Sentinel incidents.

elements.name: enableAutomationRule
elements.label: Enable automation rule (auto-trigger playbooks)
elements.toolTip: Creates an automation rule that automatically triggers the MDE and CA playbooks when incidents are created.

elements.name: enableAnalyticsRulesLibrary
elements.label: Deploy full analytics rules library (17 rules, ALL disabled by default)
elements.toolTip: Deploys the complete library of 17 SpyCloud analytics rules including: plaintext password detection, PII exposure, session cookie theft, device re-infection, multi-domain exposure, geographic anomaly, remediation gap, AV bypass, and 4 identity provider correlation rules (Okta, Duo, Ping, Entra). ALL rules deploy DISABLED — you must review and enable each one individually.

elements.name: enablePostDeployScript
elements.label: Run automated post-deployment script (resolves DCE/DCR + assigns RBAC)
elements.toolTip: Automatically runs a deployment script after all resources are created. It waits for the Sentinel content template, resolves the DCE Logs Ingestion URI and DCR Immutable ID, and assigns the Monitoring Metrics Publisher role to both Logic Apps. This eliminates the need to run scripts/post-deploy.sh manually for basic setup. MDE and Graph API permissions still require manual steps.

elements.name: enableNotifications
elements.label: Enable email/Teams notifications
elements.toolTip: Creates an Action Group and health alert that notifies you when SpyCloud data ingestion stops for more than 3 hours.

elements.name: notificationEmail
elements.label: Notification Email
elements.toolTip: Email address for alert notifications.

options.text:
Identity Provider correlation rules detect when credentials compromised in SpyCloud data are actively used to sign in through your identity providers. This catches account takeover in real-time.

**Supported providers (must be ingested into Sentinel separately):**
• Okta SSO → Content Hub → 'Okta Single Sign-On' connector → Table: Okta_CL
• Cisco Duo → Content Hub → 'Cisco Duo Security' connector → Table: Duo_CL
• Ping Identity → Syslog/API via AMA agent → Table: PingFederate_CL
• Entra ID → Diagnostic Settings → SignInLogs table (built-in)
• CyberArk → Content Hub → 'CyberArk EPM' connector → Table: CyberArk_CL

elements.name: enableSessionCookieDetection
elements.label: Enable session cookie theft detection rules
elements.toolTip: Deploys rules specifically for severity 25 exposures — stolen cookies and session tokens that enable MFA bypass.

elements.name: enableIdentityProviderAlerts
elements.label: Enable identity provider correlation rules (Okta, Duo, Ping, Entra)
elements.toolTip: Deploys rules that correlate SpyCloud data with Okta, Duo, Ping, and Entra sign-in logs. Requires those providers' logs to be ingested into Sentinel.
